service.title: Annotation Service
service.description: A service enabling users to add persistent, structured or free-text annotations to information entities such as digital assets, documents or metadata records. Annotations can include comments, tags or references and are stored in a format supporting reuse and semantic integration. The service supports collaborative work, allowing multiple users to annotate the same entity simultaneously. Hosted as a RESTful API on a Docker-compatible web server with a database backend (e.g. PostgreSQL) and optional OAuth2 authentication.

access_point.notes: RESTful API on a standard Docker-compatible web server. Database backend (e.g. PostgreSQL). Optional OAuth2 authentication.

intended_for.audience: Conservation scientists, heritage professionals and researchers requiring collaborative knowledge capture on digital content
RHDT pilot users performing structured annotation of 3D models, documents and datasets

activity.activity_label: operation_05 — Spatiotemporal Data Visualisation and VR/AR Experience: enabling expert users to create persistent annotations on the 3D model of the Japanese Tower, linking heritage documentation and analytical evidence to specific spatial locations

has_functionality.functionality: Addition of persistent structured or free-text annotations to digital assets, documents and metadata
Support for comments, tags and references as annotation types
Collaborative multi-user annotation of the same entity
Semantic storage format supporting reuse and knowledge graph integration
Transparency, traceability and long-term knowledge preservation
